text: So in this brief video, we see the skin incision and the subcutaneous division.
The incision is marked by a skin marking pen.
And this picture shows the position of the incision as we just described.
The skin is incised using a scalpel.
And then the gauze is used to make the wound dry.
And the electrocautery is used to cut the subcutaneous layer down to the muscles.
The incision is extended up to the anterior edge of the wound.
So the first muscle layer is the litmus dorsi, which is divided from the posterior part to the anterior part using the diathermy.
When the muscle is divided, we see the thoracolumbar fascia in the auscultatory triangle.
and then the anterior part of the muscle is divided.
It is elevated using forceps or the hand so that the underlying serratus anterior muscle is not divided.
The serratus fascia is carefully dissected.
Here we can see one strip of the serratus anterior.
The line of the incision of the serratus fascia is close to the serratus anterior muscle, but it should not cross the muscle.
Posteriorly, the trabezius muscle may be seen and the rhomboid muscles may be divided partially.
The scapula is elevated or retracted and the hand is used to palpate the intercostal spaces.
Usually the first space that we feel is a second intercostal space due to the attachment of the serratus posterior to the second rib.

then we use the electrocautery to divide the tissues along the superior border of the sixth strip the division is done from the back to front in the direction of the intercostal muscle fibers posteriorly we see the erector spiny muscle

and then we make a small opening in the pleura posteriorly and we check that the lung is deflated by creating a pneumothorax we complete the division of the intercostal muscle fibers from back to front
And this will ensure a clean division of the tissues at the superior border of the sixth rib.
We divide the tissues as anteriorly as possible.
And then we go to the process of division of a segment of the rib posteriorly.
using the electrocotary we mark a small segment and then we use Robert forceps to separate the tissues behind the lip and we make two marks on the segment of the lip
Then we use a rib cutter to excise the two centimeters segment from the rib posteriorly.
This will ensure or will avoid uncontrolled rib fracture when the rib spreader is placed.
The lip spreader is placed and opened slowly to give time for the tissues to expand.
It's very important.
And then using the diathermy, the incision is extended internally up to the internal memory vessels.
then the serratus anterior is retracted anteriorly and a stay suture is placed and tied on the retractor to improve exposure of the thoracic cavity.
